summary: Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood co-founder, best known for Ophelia (floating in a stream) and Christ in the House of His Parents (controversial carpentry shop scene). Married Effie Gray after her unconsummated marriage to John Ruskin. Also painted The Order of Release, Peace Concluded, and Bubbles.

works.name: Ophelia (1851–1852)
works.description: Depicts Shakespeare's Ophelia singing while floating down a stream surrounded by brightly colored flowers before she drowns. Elizabeth Siddal posed in a cold bathtub as the model. Landscape inspired by the Hogsmill River.

clues.clue: Depicts Ophelia floating/singing in a stream/river before drowning, holding scattered bouquet of flowers
examples: painted a Shakespeare character floating down a stream
had Elizabeth Siddal lie in a cold bathtub in order to paint a girl singing while floating down the river before she drowns
depicted the titular character surrounded by dark brown water and holding a scattered bouquet of brightly-colored flowers

clues.clue: Elizabeth Siddal posed in a cold bathtub; her father requested payment for medical bills after she caught a cold
examples: had Elizabeth Siddal lie in a cold bathtub
The father of that painting's model requested payment for medical bills after his daughter caught a cold

clues.clue: Landscape inspired by the Hogsmill River
examples: a landscape inspired by the Hogsmill River

works.name: Christ in the House of His Parents (1849–1850)
works.description: Controversial painting depicting Jesus in a lower-class carpentry shop with wood shavings on the floor, the red-headed boy Jesus holding up a bleeding hand, kissing his mother. A dove sits on a ladder representing the Holy Spirit. Charles Dickens criticized it. Ruskin defended it.

clues.clue: Controversial carpentry shop scene: red-headed boy Jesus holds up bleeding hand, kisses mother; wood shavings on the floor
examples: sparked a controversy over realism with a painting in which wood shavings litter the floor of a carpentry shop where a red-headed boy is tended to by his parents
the title figure has injured his hand while assisting on a carpentry project
the central boy kisses his mother and holds up a bleeding hand in a carpenter's shop

clues.clue: A dove on a ladder in the background represents the Holy Spirit
examples: represented the Holy Spirit as a dove sitting on a ladder in the background
includes a depiction of a dove resting on a ladder, which represents the Holy Spirit

clues.clue: Charles Dickens criticized the painting; John Ruskin defended it
examples: Charles Dickens had several mean things to say about this Millais painting
Ruskin defended this Pre-Raphaelite artist who showed a lower-class carpenter shop

clues.clue: John the Baptist and Mary also appear in the painting
examples: several figures around a carpentry table, including John the Baptist and Mary, as well as Jesus

clues.clue: William Holman Hunt created a companion piece: A Converted British Family Sheltering a Christian Missionary
examples: This other artist created a companion piece to Christ in the House of His Parents

works.name: The Order of Release (1853)
works.description: A woman hands the title document to a British soldier/guard as her wounded husband (arm in sling) embraces her while a dog jumps up on him. Effie Gray posed for it.

clues.clue: Woman hands the title document/paper to a guard/soldier as her wounded husband (arm in sling) holds onto her; a dog jumps up
examples: made a painting of a dog jumping up on a man with his arm in a sling who is embracing his wife, who hands a piece of paper to a guard
a woman hands the title document to a British soldier as her wounded husband holds onto her for support

works.name: Peace Concluded (1856)
works.description: Two girls play with toy animals as their father holds a newspaper announcing the end of the Crimean War. Effie Gray posed for it.

clues.clue: Two girls play with toy animals as their father holds a newspaper announcing the end of the Crimean War
examples: two girls play with toy animals as their father holds a newspaper announcing the end of the Crimean War

works.name: Other Works
works.description: Includes Bubbles (sold to Pears Soap), The North-West Passage, The Boyhood of Raleigh, The Ruling Passion (ornithologist with stuffed bird), Sir Isumbras at the Ford, The Eve of St. Agnes, Pizarro Seizing the Inca of Peru, and Princes in the Tower.

clues.clue: Bubbles: child looking up in wonder blowing bubbles; sold to Pears Soap for advertisements (commercialized Homo Bulla motif)

drew ire for commercializing the Homo Bulla motif when he sold depictions of children blowing bubbles to Pears Soap

clues.clue: The North-West Passage: painting of Trelawny as a wizened sailor, subtitle 'It might be done and England should do it'; teetotaler Trelawny outraged by a glass of grog

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Co-founder of the Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood with Dante Gabriel Rossetti and William Holman Hunt. Published ideas in The Germ. Married Effie Gray after her unconsummated marriage to John Ruskin. Frequently hired Fanny Eaton (daughter of a Jamaican slave) as a model.

comprehensive_summary:
John Everett Millais was an English painter and co-founder of the Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood, alongside Dante Gabriel Rossetti and William Holman Hunt. He is best known for Ophelia (1851-52), depicting Shakespeare's character floating in a stream surrounded by brightly colored flowers before drowning, with Elizabeth Siddal posing in a cold bathtub as the model (her father later requested payment for medical bills). The landscape was inspired by the Hogsmill River.

His other signature work, Christ in the House of His Parents (1849-50), controversially depicted Jesus as a red-headed boy in a lower-class carpentry shop with wood shavings on the floor, holding up a bleeding hand and kissing his mother, with a dove on a ladder representing the Holy Spirit. Charles Dickens criticized the painting, while John Ruskin defended it. William Holman Hunt created a companion piece.

Millais married Effie Gray after her unconsummated marriage to Ruskin, and she posed for The Order of Release (a woman handing papers to a guard while her wounded husband with an arm in a sling embraces her and a dog jumps up) and Peace Concluded (two girls play with toy animals as their father reads of the Crimean War's end). Other works include Bubbles (sold to Pears Soap, commercializing the Homo Bulla motif), The North-West Passage (portrait of Trelawny as a sailor, who was outraged by the inclusion of grog), The Boyhood of Raleigh (deconstructed on Talk Talk's album cover), The Ruling Passion (ornithologist with a stuffed bird of paradise), Sir Isumbras at the Ford (parodied by Frederic Sandys), The Eve of St. Agnes, Pizarro Seizing the Inca of Peru, and portraits of Cardinal Newman and the Princes in the Tower. The Brotherhood published in the periodical The Germ, and Millais frequently hired Fanny Eaton, daughter of a Jamaican slave, as a model.
